question: What does Figure 1.2-1 depict about the NuScale plant site layout?

nuscale_ch01_0031.title: Plant Cooling Water Systems
nuscale_ch01_0031.summary: The plant cooling water systems in the NuScale design include several nonsafety-related systems that support plant operations. These systems consist of the reactor component cooling water system (RCCWS), reactor and spent fuel pool cooling systems, and the circulating water system, which collectively manage heat removal from various plant components and pools, transferring it to the site cooling water system or cooling towers. These systems ensure effective thermal management for both operational and maintenance needs.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides an overview of the NuScale plant design, summarizing its main systems and safety features, including design criteria, emergency systems, and waste management. It explains the distinction between certified and site-specific design elements, noting that some information is representative or conceptual and marked accordingly, while security-sensitive details are withheld. Each COL applicant must reference the NuScale FSAR and provide site-specific details as required.

nuscale_ch01_0016.title: Principal Site Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0016.summary: This section provides a general overview of the NuScale plant design, describing key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, and waste management. It explains that each Combined License (COL) applicant must reference the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) and supplement it with site-specific information, while distinguishing between certified design elements and conceptual design information (CDI). Security-related details are specially marked and withheld according to regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0038.title: Main Control Room
nuscale_ch01_0038.summary: The Main Control Room (MCR) is located below grade in the Control Room Building (CRB) and includes various support spaces such as offices, conference rooms, and equipment rooms. It houses control panels for all installed NuScale Power Modules (NPMs), allowing operators to monitor and control multiple reactors using digital control systems that ensure independence between safety and nonsafety systems. The MCR provides comprehensive plant information, alarms, and controls for effective operation and diagnostics.

nuscale_ch01_0842.title: SRP 17.5, Rev 1: Quality Assurance Program Description - Design Certification, Early Site Permit and New COL applicantsII.Q Quality Assurance Records
nuscale_ch01_0842.summary: This section confirms that NuScale’s Quality Assurance Program Description for Design Certification, Early Site Permit, and new Combined License (COL) applicants fully conforms to SRP 17.5, Rev 1 requirements regarding Quality Assurance Records. No exceptions or deviations are noted, indicating complete compliance with the regulatory criteria.

nuscale_ch01_0135.title: Table 1.9-3: Conformance with NUREG-0800, Standard Review Plan (SRP) and Design Specific Review Standard (DSRS)
nuscale_ch01_0135.summary: Table 1.9-3 summarizes NuScale’s conformance with the regulatory criteria outlined in NUREG-0800 (SRP) and DSRS, indicating whether each section’s acceptance criteria are met or applicable. The table shows that NuScale generally conforms to relevant SRP sections, while certain criteria are marked not applicable as they pertain only to Combined License (COL) applicants not referencing the Design Certification Application (DCA).

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) to visually clarify design information alongside the written text. It references tables and figures listing and explaining the symbols used in these diagrams, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must provide site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation and control (I&C), and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information. It lists the relevant diagrams and legends provided for both I&C and piping and instrumentation diagrams, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be supplied by COL applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0134.title: 8.40 Methods for Measuring Effective Dose Equivalent from External Exposure
nuscale_ch01_0134.summary: Section 8.40 states that the guidance for methods of measuring effective dose equivalent from external radiation exposure is not applicable to the NuScale facility, as these dosimetry methods are the responsibility of the COL applicant or licensee. Therefore, NuScale does not address this guidance in its safety analysis report.

nuscale_ch01_0033.title: General Arrangement of Major Structures and Equipment
nuscale_ch01_0033.summary: This section describes the general arrangement of major structures and equipment at the NuScale Power Plant, focusing on the central Reactor Building (RXB). The RXB is a large, reinforced concrete structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) in a common reactor pool with individual bays arranged in two rows. The section also references detailed figures and notes that some buildings, like the administration and training building, are outside the scope of the report.

nuscale_ch01_0130.title: 1.9.5 Advanced and Evolutionary Light-Water Reactor Design Issues
nuscale_ch01_0130.summary: This section explains that the NuScale design incorporates relevant operational experience and regulatory guidance, including NRC Bulletins, Generic Letters, and 10 CFR 21 notifications, to ensure safety and compliance. It also addresses advanced and evolutionary light-water reactor design issues as recommended by NRC guidance, specifically referencing SECY-93-087 and related policy documents.

nuscale_ch01_0361.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.5 Chemical and Volume Control System Design and Arrangement
nuscale_ch01_0361.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS) design’s conformance with regulatory criteria, noting that the system generally meets requirements for design, arrangement, and leakage detection, with partial conformance regarding holdup tank venting and draining due to differences in NuScale’s design. It also clarifies that certain criteria, such as those for BWR standby liquid control, are not applicable to the NuScale PWR design.

nuscale_ch01_0067.title: Drawings and Other Detailed Information
nuscale_ch01_0067.summary: This section describes the inclusion of simplified instrumentation, control, electrical, and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information alongside written descriptions. It references tables and figures listing and explaining the symbols used in these diagrams, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must provide additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.
